alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against Codex maximum levels (MLs). Maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants are established by the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) and are legally permitted concentrations. Codex works to keep these levels as low as possible based on sound scientific evidence.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Allergen List - Mandatory allergen declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Mandatory allergen declaration requirements for seasoning, snack food, and processed foods are expected to be detailed within relevant GSO technical regulations, likely including GSO 9:2022, requiring verification of the full GSO standards database.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation sets mandatory requirements for the labelling of prepackaged foodstuffs, which includes seasoning, snack food, and other processed foods. Specific details regarding warnings, ingredient lists, nutritional information, and allergen declarations require verification of the full document.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation sets general labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Halal requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation outlines general requirements for Halal food. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation establishes microbiological limits for foodstuffs intended for human consumption and food ingredients used in the food industry. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Compliance with Food Sanitation Law
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Required. This foundational law governs all aspects of food safety, including additives, contaminants, microbiology, and labeling.

alerts.title: Added: Malaysia Allergen List - Allergen Declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. The provided text is a PDF stream and does not contain directly readable content. Food Regulations 1985, as a comprehensive food law, would mandate allergen declarations for specified allergens.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Not specified
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Food products containing non-permitted additives cannot be imported or sold. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Not specified
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Food products exceeding regulatory limits for contaminants or incidental constituents must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary Drug Residues
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of meat and food derived from meat containing diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol residues is prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Ingredients and Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory Declaration. All ingredients and additives in prepacked foods must be declared on the food label.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive.. These are 10 of the 11 mandatory allergens.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Mandatory declaration if concentration is 10 mg/kg or more.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Delisted color additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not permitted for use in food.. Delisted color additives are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from use in food.. Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) and the Substances Added to Food inventory.

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level. Specific action levels also exist for Brazil nuts, peanuts and peanut products, and pistachio nuts.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - "Gluten-free" claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must meet defined standard for gluten content.. The FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling. Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen preventive controls
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Establishments must implement written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117).

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Must be declared on the label as they are identified as allergenic substances.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The food source of a major food allergen must appear either in parentheses following the ingredient name or immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a "contains" statement. The specific type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Required to be identified on labels due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents (e.g., sodium bisulfite)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Must be declared on food labels when present at or above 10 ppm total sulfites due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.

alerts.title: Added: China Additive Limits - Food Additive Use
alerts.details: New: Verification required per GB 2760-2015. Specific additive limits for various food categories, including seasonings, snack foods, and processed foods, must be verified by consulting the full GB 2760-2015 standard document.

alerts.details: New: Verification required. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble when using this database.
